description: Integrate OMIM into terminology and translation support
known_blockers: OMIM API/download access is licensed and requires credentials.
Narrative clinical synopses and identifiers must not be redistributed beyond allowed terms.

OMIM registration, API/download credentials, and redistribution terms must be cleared before committing genemap2, morbidmap, clinical synopsis text, phenotype labels, gene-disease relationship rows, full API responses, or release payload rows.
expected_blockers: Gene-disease-phenotype evidence requires careful source attribution.
English-only coverage limits multilingual triangulation outputs.
blocker_owner: track implementer until reassigned in Phase 0
fallback_path: Use OMIM identifier references, metadata-only governance records, and access-controlled local manifests until registration, API/download credentials, and redistribution terms are confirmed.

evidence_score_policy.promotion_rule: no ontology or LLM signal promotes a translation without human review

last_run.source_version: OMIM live release not downloaded; official site/API/download routes and GitHub organization recorded
last_run.validation_command: pixi run build-ontology-network; pixi run validate-conductor; pixi run validate-ontology-network; pixi run validate-ontology-network-artifacts; pixi run test-conductor-validation; git diff --check
open_blockers: OMIM registration and credentials required before payload access
API/download terms and redistribution policy review required
release/version pinning required after authenticated access
gene-disease-phenotype evidence attribution rules required before downstream crosswalks

restricted_payload_policy.commit_policy: commit metadata, schemas, bounded non-restricted samples, validation summaries, review-pack structure, and derived reports only after source terms are verified; raw, credentialed, private, full-release, and licensed payloads stay local-only
required_evidence: local-only manifest if any restricted source is touched
redaction or exclusion proof before commit

fail_fast_probe.scope: one authoritative source record, one normalized registry row, one provenance row, one candidate comparison or crosswalk edge when applicable
success_criteria: source authority and license state recorded
payload handling path classified as commit-safe or local-only
schema validation passes for the bounded sample
review pack marks LLM-derived content as candidate-only when present
